sections.label: Section 1301
sections.original: μοῖρά τʼ ἀνάγκης ἦγʼ ἐς τὸ χρεών ,
sections.translation: Necessity ’ s fate led to what must be , and unwise speech from the mouth of Phoebus .

sections.label: Section 1303
sections.original: τίς δʼ ἔμʼ Ἀπόλλων , ποῖοι χρησμοὶ
sections.translation: But what Apollo , what sort of oracles , ordained for me to be my mother ’ s murderer ?

sections.label: Section 1305
sections.translation: The deeds were shared , the fates were shared ; one ancestral curse has ground down both .

sections.label: Section 1308
sections.original: ὦ σύγγονέ μοι , χρονίαν σʼ ἐσιδὼν
sections.translation: Ah , my sister , seeing you after a long time , at once I am robbed of your affection ,

sections.label: Section 1310
sections.translation: and I must abandon you , abandoned by you .

sections.label: Section 1311
sections.original: πόσις ἔστʼ αὐτῇ καὶ δόμος · οὐχ ἥδʼ
sections.translation: She has a husband and a home ; she does not suffer pitiably , except that she leaves the city of the Argives .

sections.label: Section 1314
sections.translation: And what other unhappiness is greater

sections.label: Section 1315
sections.translation: than to leave the boundary of one ’ s native land ?

sections.label: Section 1316
sections.translation: But I shall leave my father ’ s house , and at a stranger ’ s tribunal undergo trial for my mother ’ s murder .

sections.label: Section 1319
sections.translation: Have courage ; you will go

sections.label: Section 1320
sections.translation: to the holy town of Pallas ; only hold out .

sections.label: Section 1321
sections.translation: Clasp me to your breast , my dearest brother ; for the curse of our mother ’ s blood is separating us from our father ’ s home .

sections.label: Section 1325
sections.translation: Throw your arms in close embrace about me . Lament as if I were dead , over my grave .

sections.label: Section 1327
sections.translation: Alas ! You have said things terrible even for gods to hear . For in me and in the Olympians there is

sections.label: Section 1330
sections.translation: pity for much-suffering mortals .

sections.label: Section 1331
sections.translation: I shall no longer see you !

sections.label: Section 1332
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἐγὼ ἐς σὸν βλέφαρον πελάσω .
sections.translation: Nor will I draw near your sight !

sections.label: Section 1333
sections.translation: These are my last words to you .

sections.label: Section 1334
sections.translation: Farewell , my city !

sections.label: Section 1335
sections.translation: And a long farewell to you , my fellow-countrywomen !

sections.label: Section 1336
sections.translation: Are you going already , most faithful one ?

sections.label: Section 1337
sections.translation: I am going , my young eye wet with tears .

sections.label: Section 1340
sections.translation: Go , Pylades , and be happy ; marry Electra .

sections.label: Section 1342
sections.translation: Marriage will be for them to think of . But go towards Athens , seeking to escape these hounds of hell , for they are pursuing you fearfully ,

sections.label: Section 1345
sections.translation: the dark-skinned ones , with snakes for hands , holding a reward of dreadful pains . But we two must go in haste over the Sicilian sea to rescue the seagoing ships . As we go through the plains of the air ,

sections.label: Section 1350
sections.translation: we do not come to the aid of those who are polluted ; but we save and release from severe hardships those who love piety and justice in their ways of life . And so , let no one wish to act unjustly ,

sections.label: Section 1355
sections.translation: or set sail with perjurers ; as a god , I give this address to mortals .

sections.label: Section 1357
sections.translation: Farewell ! Any mortal who is able to fare well , and is not worn down by any misfortune , achieves happiness .
